hot_domains.notes: All 4 major platforms running PhD/expert tracks. Mercor paying $70-120/hr for physics, bio, finance PhDs. DataAnnotation advertising $50-100+/hr for domain experts. This TG is actively competed for.
DataAnnotation $50-75+/hr. Mercor $100/hr for open source. Saturated — Outlier needs strong differentiator here.
DataAnnotation $20-50+ for bilingual. Outlier historically best CPA ($2.83) in language TG.

underserved_domains.notes: DataAnnotation lists 'Clinical Researcher' role but no dedicated medical track visible. Mercor has bio PhDs but no explicit clinical track. Outlier has HLE Medical — potential whitespace.
underserved_domains.opportunity: Outlier can own clinical medicine TG before Mercor ramps medical-specific campaigns

competitor_ads.top_hook: Work whenever and wherever you want.
competitor_ads.visual_style: Clean, professional — no person photos on main site
competitor_ads.social_proof: 4.6/5 Indeed (145 reviews), MIT researcher testimonial, media logos (NYT, TIME, BuzzFeed)
competitor_ads.gap_vs_outlier: DataAnnotation does NOT mention Scale AI backing, frontier model access, or $500M+ payout total. These are Outlier exclusives.
competitor_ads.angle_saturation: C (flexibility) is their lead — avoid repeating this verbatim

competitor_ads.top_hook: Shape the future of AI. Find top-tier, remote, AI roles for your expertise.
competitor_ads.earnings_claim: $70-90/hr for physics PhD; $120/hr equity research; $100/hr open source
competitor_ads.visual_style: Profile photo thumbnails, hiring velocity stats (e.g. '42 hired recently')
competitor_ads.social_proof: $500M annual revenue, $1.5M paid out daily, OpenAI/Anthropic as customers
competitor_ads.gap_vs_outlier: Mercor had a major data breach in 2026 with 5 class-action lawsuits — active trust liability. Outlier (Scale AI) has enterprise-grade security by contrast.
competitor_ads.angle_saturation: A (expertise hook) with specialization naming. Avoid generic 'shape the future of AI' — Mercor owns it.

competitor_ads.top_hook: Make up to $150 an hour training AI chatbots.
competitor_ads.gap_vs_outlier: LinkedIn marketplace is in testing only — not yet live. Early mover advantage for Outlier on PhD/expert TG before LinkedIn activates.
competitor_ads.angle_saturation: B (earnings) — but LinkedIn is using inflated top-end claims. Outlier should use realistic $50-100/hr range with $500M proof.

top_complaints_competitors: Mercor data breach 2026 — 5 class-action lawsuits (trust liability)
DataAnnotation no email notifications for new projects — users miss work
Both platforms: accounts suspended without warning before payout
Mercor: interview-heavy onboarding (Micro1, Alignerr interviews mentioned as friction)
All platforms: work availability is unpredictable, dry spells common
top_complaints_outlier: Queue inconsistency — work arrives unpredictably, long dry spells
Linter friction — contradictory automated feedback reduces effective hourly rate
Account bans without clear reason, appeal process unresponsive
Unpaid onboarding — long training process before first paid task
top_praise_outlier: Most reliable payment — consistently pays right amount on time
Specialist rates: $70-75/hr on Project Blackbeard (specialist work)
Legitimate, mostly well-run platform backed by Scale AI
Best entry point for AI training experience
recurring_questions: Is Outlier AI legit? → ad should lead with Scale AI / $500M+ proof
How much does Outlier actually pay? → ad should show specific rate for PhD/expert tier
Will I actually get work? → ad should manage expectation or address availability

experiment_ideas.idea: Counter Mercor's data breach liability — use 'Scale AI-backed, enterprise-grade security' as subheadline trust element. Mercor has 5 active lawsuits. This is a direct differentiator.
Specialization-specific hook — name the exact research domain (HLE = hard science/math/medicine). 'Your HLE-level expertise is rare. AI labs need exactly that.' Do not use generic 'your expertise'.
Freedom from publication cycle pressure — 'Grant deadlines don't have to define your income.' Mercor and DataAnnotation are NOT running this angle. Whitespace opportunity.
experiment_ideas.source: Competitive gap — neither competitor addresses academic schedule constraint

experiment_ideas.idea: Lead with $500M paid out total — Mercor uses $500M annual revenue (internal metric), Outlier has $500M+ contractor payouts (more relatable). Same number, opposite framing.
experiment_ideas.source: Outlier differentiator vs Mercor revenue claim

competitor_hooks: Work whenever and wherever you want. (DataAnnotation)
Your domain expertise IS the training data. (DataAnnotation)
Find top-tier, remote, AI roles for your expertise. (Mercor)
Make up to $150 an hour training AI chatbots. (LinkedIn AI marketplace)
avoid: Shape the future of AI — Mercor owns this phrase
Work whenever you want — DataAnnotation owns this, too generic
Up to $150/hr — LinkedIn is inflating rates, damages trust
Side income / side hustle framing — confirmed worst performer internally

hot_tgs_by_listing_volume: PhD researchers (physics, biology, chemistry, medicine) — all platforms competing
Software engineers / Python ML — all platforms competing
Finance / equity research PhDs — Mercor leads, Outlier whitespace
Clinical / medical experts — underserved, Outlier opportunity
